commentBody: you should be able to! I would make sure to roll out your portions before freezing. If you remember, it helps to bring the dough out of the freezer to the fridge overnight. If baking straight from the freezer, you may need to add bake time and watch to make sure there is even baking.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: The cookie is great, no question. I made smaller ones - this recipe made 3 dozen. I used some milk chocolate chips as well as bar chocolate and heath bar toffee. I was so surprised that the recipe didn't warn cooks about being careful adding the vanilla. I had a big boil-over resulting in a mess and a necessary re-do of some of the butter.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody:
SOOOOO GOOOOOD! And all 5 food groups in 1 recipe: white carbs, fat, sugar, salt & chocolate. 

The only thing that was a surprise is that when I added water to my very hot, very browned butter, it reacted and rose up over the top of the container I had it in! A bit messy. But easily recovered by adding more butter to make up for what I spilled.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: These are absolutely incredible! If you do have the time, make the toffee from scratch using the recipe provided because it has an incredible depth of flavor and it melts better than commercial toffee bits. I might do semisweet or bittersweet  chocolate next time rather than milk chocolate, but that’s just a personal preference. One quick bit of advice - definitely let the butter cool before adding the vanilla. Mine had a reaction that resulted in the butter bubbling up and over the top of the measuring cup, which resulted in me having to add some additional butter to make up the difference.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: Old lady (77) home cook here who lives in the sticks. I used Ghirardelli’s baking chips and Heath chips rather than trying to make my own toffee or chop chocolate. Used a 1/4 cup measuring cup, well packed, for each cookie and came up with 22 cookies rather than 18. Let the cookie dough rest overnight in the “walk-in cooler” (screened-in porch which in the winter doubles as a walk-in cooler here in New England!).  Luckily had flaky sea salt, a gift from my daughter last Christmas, something the local supermarkets definitely do not stock. The cookies took about 22 minutes total to get to the browned-edge stage.  A total WOW!  My husband and I shared the first one, still a bit warm, got gooey chocolate all over our fingers and maybe our faces, too. Not a cookie I’d make every day, to be sure, but for a once-a-year holiday treat (and enough to gift some) they’re worth the effort. Thumbs up! Chef’s kiss!!  These do spread quite a bit, don’t try to put more than six to a baking sheet.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody:
Way too much sugar, waaaay too much! Why do Americans consume so much sugar?
I used 1 cup of dark brown sugar. And that's it.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: Can someone please sell me some? I’m not a good cook or baker but I appreciate good food.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: @Renee You can become a great baker by baking. I hope you give it a try. :)

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody:
Had to share what a friend just sent after I made these for friends:
 Lou absolutely loved your cookie last night.  I went to bed thinking about how wonderful it was.  I will say that is the best cookie I have ever had in my life.  Honestly! Thank you ❤️

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: We put the dough in the fridge before making the balls, (big mistake), so we had to let it come up to room temp before we could proceed. Made 50 gram balls and cooked for about 12 min and got 35 cookies. Well, it will be 35, we're baking them 6 at a time and keeping the balls in the freezer until needed.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: WARNING: When we added the vanilla to the still hot browned butter, it bubbled up and boiled over (even with the ice cubes in it). Take care to let the butter cool a bit first. Other tips: you'll need a very big bowl for mixing all the ingredients together. Instead of making monster cookies, we used a 2-tablespoon cookie scoop and cooked them for 13.5 minutes and that worked out nicely.  The salt is essential to balance the sweetness. Overall: excellent cookies, will make again!

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: Don't let the over 7 hours discourage you from baking these. I have made all the "best" chocolate chip cookie recipes online and have even done blind tasting with cookie snobs using top four recipes and these were the winner! Make a batch and freeze rest in balls and enjoy at your leisure. Vaughn, I'm hoping your new cookbook arrives under the tree for me this Christmas!

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: I am surprised at the addition of water or ice into the browned butter??

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: @Carol Ade Browning the butter evaporates water content so water is added back in in some recipes.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: @Carol Ade oh my God just realized I missed this when I took them out of the oven… alas! Must read more closely.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: Is there an egg substitute?  Would love to make these with my daughter, who has an allergy.  Thank you.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: @Question I've never subbed out eggs in a cookie like this but other recipes I've used applesauce, finely shredded carrots, or garbanzo bean liquid. Some folks have used flax seeds.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: @Hannah My brother uses flax seed eggs all the time in baking, including in cookies. Mix 1 T ground flax seed with 3 T water and let sit for 10 minutes or so until it becomes gelatinous.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: This cookie has it ALL! Dark, light chocolate, toffee bits, salt. The recipe is flawless. I was in a time crunch and could only chill for 2.5 hours and cookies turned out fine! However, only a few were baked. The rest went in overnight, and some for the freezer. A bit labor intensive, but worth it for the perfect cookie.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: Yum! These were worth the time and effort for sure! I think next time I’d use only dark chocolate, I like the bitterness against the very sweet cookie and toffee. I tested my dough for 4 hours and they turned out well. Next time I will allot for a 48hr rest to see how it compares!

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: On behalf of the Cookie Curmudgeon I quote his comment: “Any cookie that just uses a little dough to knit together a bunch of candy is not a real cookie”. Note: he did eat the whole giant cookie!

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody:
@Suzy K 
Hmmmm, sounds a bit hypocritical to eat the whole cookie…

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: I have been making the Jacques Torre cookie recipe, which I considered perfect, for almost two decades. So I am genuinely surprised by how wowed I was by these! They were amazing, true butterscotch flavor, soft yet chewy, and worth all the 5 star reviews. I did make my own toffee but I think they would still be incredible without it.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: I bake for my office, the first time I made these it caused a fuss because one of them decided to take the box home. The next week I made another batch and several people rushed to grab multiple cookies to take home to their families. I cannot exaggerate how much of a hit these were. But the best part is just how easy they are to make, I feel like something this popular should be more tricky but it's not. Also did the homemade toffee and I'm never bothering with store bought again.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: Hi, can I use standard milk and dark chocolate chips instead of having to find and break up from bars? I realize it’s supposed to be chunks but seems like extra work or and not sure I can find the bars. Thanks.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: @CJ I think it would be totally fine— the biggest difference will be in appearance, in my opinion. There may not be the pretty chocolate puddles on top that you’d have with the chunks, but if you are using a good quality chocolate chip, I don’t think the flavor should be that impacted.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: @CJ Chocolate chips are formulated to hold their shape, while chunks you cut yourself from a bar willl most definitely melt into pockets in the cookieand on top. The effect is different and many will tell you that affects the taste, but chips will still give you a good cookie.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: @CJ Same, I can't take the time to hand-cut the chocolate. Depending where you shop, some stores (Aldi) carry "chunks" vs chips; great for cookies.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: I followed this recipe closely and got 22 giant cookies using 1/3 cup scoop as a measure. With cookies this size and this sweet, I will likely scale by half next time unless I'm baking for a larger event. Great recipe, salt   resting well worth it!

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: What happens if you bake them without refrigeration?

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: Made it sans toffee bits since I didn't have time. I rolled the cookies but put into a smaller pan in fridge as noted--no room for a half sheet pan. I made smaller cookies--about 50 grams--which took 12 min to bake. These are amazing!!!

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: The dough by itself is heavenly. I only used 9 oz chocolate total. I’m just kind of over this trend of putting so much chocolate into cookies that it’s all you taste. The homemade toffee was definitely worth it! Sooo good and I didn’t even use a thermometer. I got 24 cookies out of this batch and thought they were pretty huge. Delicious!

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: Delicious. I was lazy and chilled without shaping. Shaped much smaller balls just before cooking, giving me about 30 cookies, and cooked for 12 mins. Perfect.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: I love this recipe.  I almost substituted butterscotch chips and thought better of it… I'm glad I invested the time and making the toffee which was actually quite easy to do. Every time I've made the recipe and shared the cookies. The recipients are beside themselves… Don't change a thing!

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: These are fantastic! I made them almost exactly as the recipe states. I did a little less chocolate chips just as a personal preference. The homemade toffee is excellent. I like my cookies crisp and thin so I dont always rest the dough, but doing it here was awesome. Some of the best cookies I have made/eaten. My mother offered to pay me to make more haha

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: Best cookie ever? I’m not sure about that. My only alteration was to make the cookies smaller — approx. 60 grams rather than the suggested 100. Reduced cooking time to around 12 minutes (from frozen).

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody:
My first attempt with the brown butter and ice cubes led to a countertop Vesuvius :) For the second attempt, I just used cold water -- much better. The smell of the vanilla in the slightly cooled brown butter was heavenly!

I reduced the size of each cookie to 1/8 cup for a yield of 36 cookies. I baked for 13 minutes and didn't get too much spread. The final product had a really nice layered effect. Really nice flavoring, looking forward to making again!

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: @Caroline I had the same volcanic effect when I added cold water.  It erupted & flowed all over the counter, down the drawers to the floor- 1/4 cup of melted, browned butter, gone!  And you know some also leaked into the drawers. What a mess!! I melted another 1/4 cup to replace it but the dough seems way too buttery to me; we’ll see how they bake up.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: One of the best cookies I’ve ever baked. But be careful: I got rave reviews when I baked them over the holidays for one side of the family, so then I had to bake them for the other side. More rave reviews. Then my neighbors. Now I’m popular. Then my wife just asked for them on a random weeknight. WILL IT EVER END?!

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: DO NOT ADD THE VANILLA EXTRACT DIRECTLY TO THE HOT BUTTER. IT WILL BOIL OVER AND DUMP THE BUTTER ALL OVER YOUR COUNTER. Why the recipe does not mention this VERY CRUCIAL piece of information is completely beyond me.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: This was delicious one thing is that the brown butter exploded after putting an ice cube in. I would recommend waiting longer than you think.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: Hi everyone. I’m making these today to bake tomorrow but I have one question. Do any of you find these too sweet? I am a big proponent of following recipes exactly. However, I’d like to know if it would make sense to add more dark chocolate chunks and fewer milk chocolate chunks. If not, I’m all in either way. Thank you for your advice.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: @Christopher S. The salt really cuts through and the homemade toffee isn’t fake sweet like store bought toffee chips. Delightful!

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: @Christopher S. I wouldn’t call them too sweet, but they are very rich with the two chocolates and toffee.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody:
Oh, Vaughn.  Sweet, affable talented Vaughn...thank you for this recipe!! I made it exactly as written (weighing everything including the dough balls) and the 3 adult men in my house have no notes! 
For my taste however,  they came out juuuuust a little too crunchy on perimeters at 18 minutes (hot oven, I guess) so will try a little less time in the oven.  And I will try subbing toasted pecans for the milk chocolate, because it was a bit too sweet for me with the toffee which MUST stay.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody:
Best cookies!  Made mine GF with King Arthur GF flour. Reduced butter by 30% as 
per Katarina Cermelj's "The Elements of Baking". (GF flours will spread out more, so they need less fat) Used Heath bar toffee bits, so I reduced the chocolate to around 3 oz each since the Heath bits have chocolate too.  Rolled into two logs in parchment and chilled overnight.  Cut one log into 1/4" slices. Had 21 "normal" sized cookies that cooked in about 10 minutes.  Happy with 40 regular sized cookies.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: I bake a lot, and can say without a doubt that these are the BEST cookies I have ever made!  I gave some away and heard comments like this:  "OMG--I couldn't stop eating them and was literally moaning with every bite!" Thank you, Vaughn!

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: These are fantastic. Worth the extra steps. I made the toffee myself (my first attempt) and accidently went past the intended temperature, so it ended up and a little bitter/smoky, almost like when you burn a marshmallow over a campfire. I was going to toss it, but it actually ended up giving the cookies a nice complexity that I wasn't mad about.  I let the dough rest overnight in the fridge and took it out two hours before baking to soften it for easier portioning. 10/10

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: Definitely some awesome cookies. Have made these twice now sticking as much as possible to the recipe. However, like a number of the comments, I found them to be quite large. And 18 to 20 minutes was not nearly enough cooking time. Ended up cooking them about 25 minutes and they still came out nice and chewy. Next time I think I’ll make them a bit smaller so they’re a little more manageable to eat and then maybe the 18 to 20 minute cooking time will be correct.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody:
I knew they would be sickeningly sweet with that much brown and white sugar.  Reduced the brown to 1 cup and the white to 2/3 cup.  Wasn't missed and could still reduce the white to 1/2 cup.
Homemade toffee didn't stand out and the milk chocolate added nothing. The milk chocolate was overrun by the dark.
Same result & less effort: Buy Heath or Skor bars and break them into pieces. Crushing bars would give larger pieces than buying toffee bits and the milk chocolate would be covered as well.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody:
For someone who is sure they will find a perfectly normal amount of sugar in a recipe "sickening" you sure do seem to like commercial candy bars, which are wayyyy sweeter than this homemade toffee recipe. Pick a lane.
If you found this recipe not so good, it was probably because you reduced the sugar to a point where the flavors were muted.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: @person. Aim for balance between salt, fat & sweet. Not logical the sugar reduction muted the flavor. 5 ingredients in this recipe signal sweet to tastebuds.  To enhance the nuance of all the flavors, adding less sugar would make the toffee & chocolates shine. There is no way a couple of Heath bars have more sugar than homemade toffee, milk choc & the reduced sugar. Leaving ‘my lane’ and will swing by to give you a ride to a food science class…or you could just look up the term 'bliss point"

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: This recipe is a “hit out of the ballpark!” I have replaced my regular chocolate chip cookie with this one. Made it as written first time (they were eaten as fast as I baked them) and now I’ve replaced the chopped chocolates with milk and semisweet chocolate chips for ease of prep (and cost!) Excellent cookie!

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: Bake time of 18-20 minutes is way too long.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: These are the best cookies on the planet. Unbelievable.  Perfect balance between crunchy and gooey, salty and sweet.  My search for the perfect cookie has ended. There is no need to try any other recipe. They’re magical.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: These are AMAZING. The neighbors, who got them as gifts, agreed. Make the toffee, it's worth it. I made about 16 of the large ones and then used a 2 TBSP scoop for about 18 more. The large chocolate bars from TJ's are perfect for this. Watch the video too.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: Amazing cookie!  i did them at a smaller size - 50g each - and baked them from the freezer for about 18 minutes.  Crispy on the edges and chewy in the center.  I dont love milk chocolate so I did all dark and it is wonderful.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: Have made this recipe 4 times now, and multiple people have said it’s the best cookie they’ve ever had. I also agree! 😋

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: 4 stars... but more as a ribbing. This cookie dough, in raw form, gets "6" stars!  And since I can only give a total of 5 stars, the cookies get 4, average is 5!.  The dough is so good I save it in shaped balls and eat it straight out of the freezer.  Just amazingly delicious.  I definitely make the cookies, also delicious.  Enjoy the batter!!!

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: Am I the only one who doesn’t get adding so much salt to all these sweet things?  I know, leave it out.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: @Sheila F — Salt makes everything more delicious. I can immediately tell when a baked good from a bakery does not include salt— it tastes bland. This is especially the case, in my opinion, for things like pie crust which tastes way more buttery when salt is included— the flavor becomes amplified. I think Samin Nosrat’s book Salt, Fat, Acid, Heat will do a much better job of explaining this, if you indeed are interested in the science and information behind this. The other thing is— the recipe calls for salted butter, but does not include any added salt in the batter. It does suggest sprinkling flaky salt on top, which adds a pop of flavor and texture. But, you could just use unsalted butter and not sprinkle salt on the top. Also, salted caramel is a classic and much more beloved than unsalted caramel because the sweet salty contrast is delicious for many people— it’s the same idea here, except with butterscotch. The salt is a nice contrast to the sweetness of the butterscotch, in the same way that the dark chocolate’s bitterness provides a nice contrast to the sweetness. You could always try making two batches of identical cookies— one salted and one unsalted to compare for yourself— see which is more delicious for you and anyone with whom you share them.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: @Sheila F I agree entirely. I’m all for the addition of salt to the dough or batter of baked goods to enhance flavor, but I find the domineering chunks and flakes of sea salt sprinkled on top are not acting as flavor enhancers, they’re just there to make the item super salty. If I wanted that much saltiness I’d have a salt lick for dessert.🐮

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody:
@Sheila F
You are definitely not the only one. When I first heard that people were putting salt on chocolate and on caramel I thought they were crazy but apparently I’m the one because I can’t even imagine adding salt to any sweet things - or bacon for that matter. Bacon ice cream - thanks I’ll pass! Part of it undoubtedly has to do with having to watch my salt intake for my health and largely eating and cooking without salt for years. I know it’s shocking but it can actually be done. With very delicious food as the result by the way. Obviously it’s impossible to completely eliminate salt from any diet and I’m not that fanatic. But things that others find normal I often find very salty. And don’t get me started on the amount of salt intake in anything prepared you buy in the store - it’s not uncommon to see frozen foods and canned soup with almost the entire daily recommended amount  of salt intake in one serving! People are obsessed with fat when it’s salt that is an equal if not more dangerous ingredient in we eat. And many foods increase the salt to compensate for the loss of flavor from reduced fat. I read the labels for everything and it is a regular cringfest! So no, I am definitely not telling anyone else what to eat or not eat - that is a perfectly individual decision. But salt on chocolate chip cookies and cupcakes! Just not my thing and good to know I’m not the only one!

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: Nearly exactly the same as the Brown Butter Toffee Chocolate Chip Cookie from Bon Appetit that has been floating around since at least 2018. Nothing new under the sun.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: I just compared the two recipes and they're actually quite different.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: @PS I have made both recipes and I have never had the number of compliments about the BA cookies as I have with these. These are next level chocolate chip cookies.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody:
Once my butter browned and cooled, I had exactly one and quarter cup, so added no more water.
I couldn’t make these with a whisk, in fact once I started adding flour I had to transfer my mixture to my standing mixer to finish the recipe. I packed the dough into a quarter cup measuring cup and it was only 75 grams, so I made my dough balls that weight, they were huge.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: @Jeff you forgot to mention how they came out!

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody:
Winner winner! Worth the (easy) extra step of making the toffee.
Baked some around 24 hr mark and will bake the rest closer to the 48 hr mark to see if the extra rest makes a difference.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: A question for cookie bakers more experienced than me: if I want to make these twelve to a baking sheet instead of six how do I adjust the baking time?

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: @Bob Boester Unless you have a massive sheet pan in a massive oven I wouldn’t do these 12 to a sheet. They will spread together into an indistinguishable mess. If you only have one sheet pan do one batch of 6 at a time, letting the sheet pan cool between batches (run it under cold water).

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody:
@Bob Boester 
The cookies are too large to bake 12 on one sheet pan. You would wind up with one very large odd shaped cookie. If you have only one sheet pan do two bakes letting pan cool in between

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: My husband and I finally agreed on the perfect cookie.. and this is it! We made the toffee and followed the recipe exactly and would make no changes. Making the cookies from the fridge made for a slightly underbaked center and crispy edges, just perfect!

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: This recipe calls for a certain amount of toffee but the recipe it includes doesn't quite make that amount. It is a 19 g discrepancy. Is that close enough or should I make double the toffee and just have lot left over?

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody:
THANK YOU from the bottom of my 56 year baking adventurous heart for embracing SALTED butter.  Seriously, thank you!!!

Can't wait to try these.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: So water into basically boiling butter equaled butter boiling over the measuring cup, so I lost some butter. But how can this not have happened to everyone?

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: This was the best cookie recipe I’ve ever made, in a lifetime love affair with the chocolate chip. Remarkably easy. Definitely make the toffee.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: These were amazing! The only change I made was I added crumbled Butterfinger. Yummy!

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: These cookies are delicious - the toffee really makes them! A couple of things I wish I’d known beforehand were making both the toffee and browned butter in a wider saucepan to allow for more even cooking. I had to make the toffee three times - first time took it to 290 and it was burned, second followed the time requirements and it was too soft and wouldn’t harden, the third time I cooked it in a larger saucepan for much less time to 290 until it just started to smell as if it was burning and it was perfect. Also, regret not reading to make the balls of dough before resting.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: I am always in search of the best chocolate chip cookie and this might be on my top 3. Used around 80g less chocolate (in total) and they were still chocolaty enough for me. Also next time I might make them a tad smaller. Baked some after 24 hrs and the rest i put in the freezer. Loved the crunchy bits from the caramelized toffee, will probably use toffee bits in all my cookies from now on

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: I had to add almost 1/2 cup ice cubes to get 1 1/4  cup liquid. After following the rest of the recipe I have a super sweet cake batter. Not sure where I went wrong,.. but not the outcome I hoped for.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: Pretty good cookie. A bit of work. I made the homemade toffee which was fun (I had no clue how easy it is to make!) I was hoping it would rival the Jacques Torres cookie for me but I prefer those. It’s big and beautiful but not quite as tasty for me and my family as I would like.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: I feel the same. I was very excited to make these and am underwhelmed by the results. JT cookies are hard to beat.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: How can I adapt this recipe for high altitude baking?

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: I wasn’t sure how well I’d like these, because I don’t love mill chocolate and was neutral on the toffee. Holy cow, I’m so glad I made them! They’re just as great as they look and it’s worth making the homemade toffee. I only had enough AP flour for half the amount, so subbed bread flour for the other half. Added a couple tbsp of milk and baked without the rest so the dough wouldn’t get too dry and they came out fantastic. Can’t wait to make them again as written - I’m sure they’ll (somehow) be even better!

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: These are amazing. I used Heath bar bits because I was too lazy to make toffee. Next time I will make the effort as the toffee flavor was not strong enough IMO. I rolled into a log before refrigerating and sliced to bake. If you actually make 18 cookies as the recipe calls for they are HUGE cookies. Next time I’ll make two smaller rolls as I don’t have enough willpower to not eat the whole big cookie.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: Amazing recipe. Next time I'm going to add in some coconut.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: These were delicious, but they spread a lot and became quite thin cookies (even after 24 hours in the fridge). Any tips?

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: The cookie recipe I have been searching for!! This is the perfect balance of sweet, salty, deep flavours (thanks to the toffee and browned butter). I would definitely say that the sprinkle of flaky salt after baking is mandatory since the dough is not particularly salty. I tried baking from cold and from freezer as described and both worked great. The effort to reward ratio is low: not overly difficult but excellent result!

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: I don't think I have ever left a comment before - but these cookies...my oh my! I made them GF and just subbed in Cup4Cup. Absolutely out of this world! I didn't have time to make my own toffee (just used pre-made), but that will definitely be attempted the next time I make them...which will most definitely be very (very) soon.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: Wow these were huge and gorgeous! And the first two bites fabulous, but as I forged on the whole thing was too overwhelmingly sweet for me. As I avoid extra salt in my diet, Amy did not add it on top, and she said that would make all the difference - maybe so.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: Really superb cookie. It bakes up browner than standard chocolate chip cookies, so watch the edges carefully or they'll overbake. Killjoy, I know, but I'll leave out the toffee next time, because like all toffee, it sticks to the teeth.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: I must have done something wrong along the way. The dough was more like a batter before I put it in the fridge. Putting ice cubes in the hot browned butter didn’t melt the ice, it just caused each ice cube to be encased in a greasy glob of congealed butter.  I had to microwave it to get the ice to melt. I left the dough in the fridge for close to 24 hours; by that time it was solid enough to scoop. But the cookies came out incredibly flat, almost no rise; what rise there was deflated almost immediately during cooling. The product was so disappointing.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: @WF the ice makes no sense! Now I just have globby chunks of semi solidified butter. And even with 2 ice cubes and water, it did not make 1 1/4 cup liquid. I should have listened to my recipe tester gut and ignored the ice cube suggestion.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: @WF watch the video. He adds the ice to the hot pan with the butter (not the liquid measuring cup) — that makes more sense.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: I had the same result.  Super thin dough that was more like a thick cake batter that created incredibly unattractive cookies that spread horribly. My cookies have been in the fridge for over 24 hours as well. I am a seasoned baker, watched the video, used a scale, and followed the recipe exactly, and I cannot for the life of me figure out how I got the end result that I did.   A lot of expensive ingredients going in the compost bin :(

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: These were great. We doubled the recipe and, feeling impatient when we saw the chilling time, froze the cookie dough in 1/4 cup (18 bakery style large) and 1/8 cup (normal size home cookies) portions for 1hour. We baked straight from the freezer according to the directions and they turned out beautifully.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody:
Pretty amazing if you ask me. I made the cookies with See’s Peanut Brittle instead of the homemade toffee. … i had tried making the toffee and mine turned out hard and slightly burnt tasting; so See’s came to the rescue. Plus I liked the taste of the peanuts. 
-And made mine 35 grams per cookie. 
-I did not get much spread when baking. I wonder if I left them in the fridge too long? mine chilled for about 30 hours. 
-Last note is that I think it would be nice to try adding cinnamon to a future batch; it’s good in chocolate chip cookies.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: I just want to say that people go nuts for these cookies. I brought them to a potluck and one of the dads was all, “this is maybe one of the best cookies of my life.” I brought some to my kid’s after school teachers and as I was walking in I saw the secretary staring at the cookies so I gave her one. As I was walking down the hall, I heard her behind me going, “ Oh my god! Oh my  god!” as she ate a cookie. The cookies are that good! Also don’t be nervous about making the toffee. I don’t have a candy thermometer and just cooked it for 8 min and it came out great

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody:
Amazing! All the stars! Can’t believe how good this cookie turned out, AND that I learned how to make toffee. Adding to my all time favorites - thank you Vaughn!! 

I froze the dough in ball sized portions per the recipe instructions. Can confirm that the dough bakes well from frozen without adjusting time or temperature!

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: I love you, Vaughn. This recipe is perfect.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: The cookies are enormous: 5-6” diameter. It’s an awesome size for a special occasion and the impressive factor, but if you need to make these for a bigger crowd, you could chop the candy pieces a bit smaller and do 2-3x as many cookies without scaling the recipe. Other readers have mentioned baking times for smaller sizes. I’ll start with those when I decide to make more and bake a few test cookies first to get it right. As a treat for my mom, kids, and a few friends, though, as-is was great!

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: Instead of scooping then chilling, I rolled into 3 equal, fat rolls (~3.5” diameter), wrapped in plastic wrap, and chilled. Then I cut each roll into 6 equal slices for baking. It worked great and hogged less room in the fridge.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: Not sure what happened when browning the butter. Transferred to Pyrex measuring cup, added ice cubes, and the mixture boiled over. The butter was perfectly browned too :(

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: @Kiki. Same here. A warning note would be nice.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: These cookies are amazing. A couple of notes, first, thank you to Vaughn Vreeland. I followed the directions step by step, down to measuring my dough balls to 100g, and they were wonderful. I got 18 cookies, my yield has never matched the recipes' before and I love that I didn't need to use my mixer. I used a bag of Bit O Brickle, next time I will use a bag of mini Heath bars and chop them up so I can have some big and little pieces. Bit O Brickle are really too little but still delish!

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: Good cookie, but not enough better than Tollhouse to justify the extra work.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: Rolled into log and chilled, make about 4 doz normal-generously sized cookies, which baked for me for 13-15 min at 350.  Wish they were a little softer, but a nice, relatively simple chocolate chip cookie variant.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody:
I managed to get 35 nice size cookies out of this recipe, baked for 18 minutes (cold out of the fridge after a 16 hr rest). 
If you make this recipe with homemade toffee you’ll have just enough salted butter left for a second batch of toffee to top with any leftover chocolate. Yum.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody:
THIS is the cookie! Has it all! 
I made the toffee w/o a thermometer - mine was soft, will get a thermometer. 

Thanks for the recipe, will look at your book!

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: this is a fabulous recipe. it is very easy and delivers a bakery worthy cookie. highly recommend.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody:
Wanted to add that these cookies are really (really) good, and if you’re going to make a recipe that calls for browned butter and an overnight chilling period, go ahead and make the toffee yourself. It’s worth it.
Also, Vaughn’s instruction video is darling!

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody:
Perfect.  Made them exactly as written then scooped into dough balls for chilling.
Don't skip the cooling on a rack step. It's key.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody:
My daughter and I baked these last night and they are delicious!!
It is worth it to make the homemade toffee which really adds a special taste that makes it have a rich butterscotch caramel flavor.
I don't normally use salted butter but we followed the recipe exactly and they were perfect.
We will definitely make these again, they are fantastic!!

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: I've never made toffee before. I watched VV's video twice before I made the toffee and cookies. Really simple and reduced my fear of candy making. After making dough, I portioned them into 40 gram balls, placed on cookie pan with parchment paper and flash froze uncovered for about 30 minutes.  Then I placed the semi frozen balls into one of those silicone Stasher bags in the fridge until ready. Baked a few early, b/c I couldn't wait. Then froze the rest in the Stasher until needed. Yum!

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: These are very good (a little too much chocolate for my taste tbh), but I had an issue with the toffee that I hope someone here knows the answer to: I made the toffee bits according to the recipe provided and they seem to have come out right. Unfortunately, when I baked the cookies, the toffee bits liquified and poured out of the cookies, creating puddles of burnt sugar (and I mean really burnt, not tasty burnt) around the cookie edges. Did I mess up the toffee somehow? Or is it something else?

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: Same has happened to me in these and other cookies I made with the homemade toffee.  I think it speaks to making sure the toffee bits are on the smaller side.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: I made these this week and they got rave reviews from everyone who had one.  However mine didn’t spread out like the ones in the video.  They came out little mounds.  They did spread a little but not to the thinnest like the author’s. I made them according to the recipe.  The only thing I did differently is that I used a 1/4 cup measuring cup instead of a 1/4 cup scoop.  Would there be that much difference to account for my mounded cookies? They were still very good, just looked different.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: Made as directed except used semi sweet chocolate chips as that was already in the pantry.  Cooked a batch after 7 hours of rest.  Turned out good, but 18 minutes was too long.   Using a little less than qtr cup per cookie they came out to about 4 in diameter.  Too large for me.   I then made a batch with 24 hours of rest, but used a regular cookie scoop to make about golf ball size.  The second batch was much better.  Deeper butterscotch flavor, and the smaller size baked more evenly.    The smaller cookies only took 9-10 minutes.  Pull them out when they still look a little raw in the center and salt immediately, otherwise it doesn’t really stick. I did make the homemade toffee and it makes a delicious candy like spot on the cookies as they bake.  They are very sweet, and I think I’d like them better using all dark chocolate to balance the sweetness.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: This recipe gives more depth than your boyfriend does, but slightly less than your English professor did. Maybe that should be reversed. Either way, cookies do exactly what they’re supposed to, nothing less.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: @Sheila F Sprinkling salt on sweets like a garnish is very trendy right now. I usually put salt in the dough and leave the salt off the top.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: I had to make a few small tweaks based on what I had. I used 100ish grams of icing sugar because I ran out of granulated and do the granulated/molasses mix for brown. I also swapped out 50g of all purpose for whole wheat to add to the earthy nutty flavor and get some extra chew hopefully. Then I only used 400g of 72% dark chocolate chopped up. I like a less sweet cookie but I think that was a step too far. I'd probably knock that down to 250-300g.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: I dislike milk chocolate.  What would make the best substitute?  More dark, white chocolate, nuts?

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody:
Are these cookies goid to mail or are they too fragile
?

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: I definitely think they'd be find to mail once they've completely cooled. I'd place some parchment paper between each layer, pack well and send!

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: @Mary Mc I'd definitely use bubble wrap between layers and also to line the inside of box.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: Do you need to let the browned butter cool before mixing in other ingredients?

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody:
@Mia

I would let the butter cool in the fridge to avoid cooking the eggs. Serious Eats has a choc chip cookie recipe I make all the time and they suggest cooling the browned butter until you see some solids on the very outside rim. It takes 20-30 mins in the fridge.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: Has anyone tried to make these into bars?

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: Has anyone made these who lives at high altitude?  I am at 7500 ft and am a little concerned about the toffee (never made it at sea level much less 'up' here).  Thanks

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: I am at 4700 feet; the guideline I've followed which google confirms is to reduce the target temp by 2 degrees F for every 1000 feet above sea level.  Has worked well for us!

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: @Liz toffee was fine but the cookie spread a lot. Next time I’ll add more flour, I’m in Denver

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: Currently addicted to these cookies. Got afraid of getting a bitter aftertaste (which didn’t happened at the end) since my toffee turned pretty dark brown to reach the recommended 290°F but I guess it’s because not all dark brown sugars are created equal. Apart from that, you’ll need Vaughn’s “guns” (aka biceps) to be able to distribute evenly the mix-ins when mixing by hand (thank you, bf).

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: While these cookies are very good, the ingredients make them expensive.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: Easy recipe to follow and nice not to have to break out anything to plug in. My cookies did spread and I used the tip on the video to use a round cookie cutter to improve the look. While they look perfect now , you lose the crispy edges on the cookies I used the cutter on. Will put into my holiday rotation .

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: Quite a fussy recipe but fortunately very forgiving and well worth the effort!  I'll admit I accidentally added the dry ingredients to the sugars before the butter but it didn't seem to matter.  I just mixed in the butter & eggs, added my homemade toffee (excellent decision!) and about 8 oz chocolate chips because that was all I had on hand.  It was plenty of chocolate and the first 7 turned out great after a 4 hour rest.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: Adding the  brown sugar, white sugar, and sugar from the toffee totals 590g. Add to that the sugar in the chocolate. I have a sweet tooth but this seems like a staggering amount of sugar. Over 30g per cookie.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: I made these to the letter, including the homemade toffee, and … first: ack so much chocolate! And the chunks just kind of fell out of the batter… I chilled it after mixing in toffee and chunks, but put in the fridge still in the bowl (should have read comments first) and then when I “scooped” balls they were rock hard and didn’t spread at all when baking. Ended up with very cake-y cookies… not sure where I went wrong except for not scooping onto the tray before chilling? I just didn’t have the fridge room for that… flavor was great but nothing like the rave reviews here and not sure it was worth the extra time and effort? Please tell me where I went wrong…

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: Did you weigh your ingredients? If not, too much flour could be why they were cakey. I thought these cookies were some of the best I've ever had so maybe try once more?

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: Chocolate Chip Cookies with an ice cold glass of milk….Heavenly….Chocolate Chip cookies says Mom…Miss you Mom…

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody:
This could come across as Scrooge-like but given the epidemic of diabetes related disease on the developed world, would it be good to add the calorie , sugar and fat etc content of the NYT recipes. Having said that, given the ecstatic comments, I’ll forward it to my American daughter in law for next time we visit ;-)

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: Watched the video and followed the recipe exactly as written, but found this to be a dud... maybe it's the impact melted butter had vs the normal creaming process, but these were dry and cakey.  I love toffee, so thought these would be a hit, but will stick to a more standard recipe.

recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: @ron you can make dough balls just after mixing the dough while it's still soft, pile them up in a bowl with a lid or a bowl covered with plastic and let them rest/chill in the fridge that way. The next day they are easy to separate and bake.

author: Vaughn Vreeland

commentBody: Takes a lot to wow me, but these cookies are so yummy. Made no modifications, but I did opt for the Heath bits instead of making my own since I had them in the pantry. Baked half and froze half for a cookie fix when the mood strikes. Make these cookies!

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)

author: Vaughn Vreeland

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: OMG to die for!  I made homemade toffee for this (really not hard).  Followed the recipe.  My only change was to make the cookies a bit smaller--about 75 grams each instead of 100.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: These were a big hit. Resist the temptation to get store-bought toffee. Vaughn's recipe for it is easy and takes no time at all. But beware! Don't make the toffee the day before because it vanishes overnight! The only thing I did differently was to make the cookies smaller. I think the 1/4 cup scoop results in a cookie that's way too big. Just a personal preference.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: I feel like these are actually even better when cooked from frozen than from the 72 hour refrigerated dough! My batch in general is a little puffier/spreads less than the pictures, though I may have added a teeny bit too much baking soda (got distracted and added 1 tbs to the flour before trying to remove it and replace with correct amount).

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: These are crazy good. Definitely recommend a 48 hour rest, if you can stand to wait, to let the flavors meld.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: Would submit that there's enough sugar in the chips and to avoid added sugar altogether (in all things!!).  And even then, to treat sweets as an indulgence rather then a habit

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: This cookie is magic in your mouth. Perfectly sweet, a touch salty, chewy and delicious!

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: Wow wow wow make these cookies!

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: These are superlative. Almost like eating a candy bar—in terms of the textures (crunchy! Crispy! Gooey!) and flavors (chocolate! Butterscotch! Salt!) Definitely the best cookie I’ve ever made, possibly the best I’ve ever eaten.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: Crowd favorite! I used 3/4 dark chocolate but kept the 12 oz total. 92 gram dough balls (15 min @350) yielded perfect, large single-serving size cookies.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: Unfortunately the tip does not adjust for half or double recipe. Beware.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: Great cookies! Home made toffee is definitely worth it (I threw out the store bought stuff). I'm not a fan of milk chocolate so I changed the ratio of milk chocolate:dark chocolate to 3 oz milk with 9 oz dark. Perhaps I will go with all dark chocolate next time.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: Did not have time to make toffee. Used 1 bag Heath Bar pieces, 1 cup semi sweet mini chips and 1 cup chunks. Also tossed in ~ 1cup chopped pecans. Used 1/4 c scoop, refrigerated dough balls overnight. Let dough balls warm up as oven came to temp. Flattened dough balls with a heavy glass. Made 25 cookies. Baked 15 min. Really liked crispy edges.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: For Vaughn Vreeland (or anyone....): How long can the dough stay in the freezer and still be good? Am thinking of preparing dough now and freezing for late December holidays. Thanks!

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: I bake a lot of cookies. A lot. And I have to say that this is a standout recipe that yields a sublime chocolate chip cookie. Don't skip making the toffee (which I had to do twice as mine initially got too hot too fast and scorched). That toffee on its own is pretty incredible, but when broken up and added to this batter rich with brown butter and dark brown sugar it's out of this world. My husband declared these to be the absolute best cookies I have ever made, so my thanks to Vaughn!!!

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: Made these for the first time and they were very good and a lot of people liked them, but I think next time I make them I’m gonna cut the toffee bits in half. I thought the cookies were too sweet and all you could taste was toffee. You couldn’t even taste the chocolate.

author: Vaughn Vreeland

author: Vaughn Vreeland

author: Vaughn Vreeland

author: Vaughn Vreeland

commentBody: For people who made this, did you use the homemade or store bought toffee?  What was the texture like after baking the cookies?  I’m trying to figure out if this recipe would be ok for people with braces.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: I made the first tray as described in the recipe and the cookies were too big for our taste. I'd rolled the dough into logs approx 2" x 10", so I cut the logs in 3/4" slices, cut each slice into 3 pieces, and baked as directed. The resulting cookies are about 2" in diameter. When I baked the next batch,  I used my shaping method and it yielded about 80 cookies. My daughter says this is her husband's new favorite cookie!

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: I've made these cookies twice this week. I used English muffin rings to keep the shape of perfect cookie. My friends loved them.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: The homemade toffee is a must! Followed the recipe to a T, and they turned out great. Would definitely make them again :)

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: I just realised 1 used 1/2 the amount  of chocolate. But I also made some more tweaks- I used 10% less sugar and swapped the tofee bits for mini marshmallows and caramelised milk protein (which I made by heating evaporated milk in butter/margarine till most of the liquid is gone and you are left with toasty brown granules). Received great feedback on the final product.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: I did not see the purpose for chilling the dough, unless you wanted to make them later. It was harder to scoop them and they did not flatten like the picture. They stayed like little hard rocks for me. If I make them again I would not chill them.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody:
The instructions direct you to chill the dough after you scoop them out, not before. 
You can read in a million places why chilling/resting is best, but the main reasons are that the cold rest hydrates the flour more evenly and results in a much nicer texture. The butterscotch flavor also deepens with chilling.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: Huge and I mean huge hit on Thanksgiving!

author: Vaughn Vreeland
recipeTitle: Salted Butterscotch Chocolate Chunk Cookies Recipe (with Video)
commentBody: I’ve made these many times with various different ingredients: white chocolate chips/dark chocolate chips; crystallized ginger/chopped apricots; dried apricots/cranberries. I’ve even added chopped rosemary or a little Fiori di Sicilia. Always a hit! Am I the only one who has an explosion when I added the vanilla to the hot butter ?
