commentBody:
2 t instant coffee
2 t sugar (allulose)
2 T water

Blend until thick and foamy. Pour over ice into a tall glass. Add 2/3 C water and cream, then stir to blend

author: Vaughn Vreeland
commentBody: I put the coffee and sugar in a small mason jar, added the hot water and the lid and shook the heck out of the jar, then poured over iced milk.  Delicious.  I did use only one packet of Verve Coffee Roasters Holiday blend.  I also agree that the Mount Hagen organic instant coffee is quite good, and certainly would have used that if I had it.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
commentBody: anyone try it without sugar (or any sweetener)? we don't like our coffee sweet.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
commentBody: Yes, Made one cup with sugar but couldnt drink because I don’t like sugar in my coffee. Remade without sugar. It didn’t get quite as frothy (I used a hand blender) but still worked quite well. Delicious.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
commentBody: This is crazy. It works. I really couldn’t imagine how it foams — not that you’d lead us astray — but where’s the fat? It is a stiff foam, like whipped cream! Wild! I use maple sugar in my coffee and did so here. Yum, indeed! And, I put it over my homemade cold brew with cream, because, why wouldn’t I?? Actually not too much. Have half left and will warm milk and use as suggested! This is a keeper! I’m buzzin’ now! Got things to do! Look out!

author: Vaughn Vreeland
commentBody: It just didn't work for me; it *sounded* good, and *looked* good. But I stood and I stood and I stood, beating it with a hand mixer, and nothing happened; how long is it supposed to take? I ran out of patience and just decided to do my usual French press over steamed milk, which is a lot less work and mess.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
commentBody:
Note that the 1/2 Tbsp sugar in the article = the 1.5 tsp in the recipe.
Achieved a thick froth, but couldn't reach the creamy stage with 1/2 Tbsp sugar - will try again with more sugar.
Fun!

author: Vaughn Vreeland
commentBody: If you enjoy coffee this is not for you. Yet another sugary American creation that explains our health crisis.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
commentBody: It’s South Korean but yes it is quite sweet!

author: Vaughn Vreeland
commentBody: @Real David Art it still works without sugar but needs more elbow to whip up.  1/2 tsp of sugar is my fit over hot milk.  Zero sugar over ice cream or gelato.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
commentBody:
Very fun to make - whips up very easily with hand mixer and it's fun to watch it go from foam to creamy as color gets lighter and lighter. One-half tablespoon of sugar is sweet enough. 
But... it doesn't taste that great. The part that's mixed with milk is best. The cream just tastes like instant coffee. Maybe it's  better depending on what brand of coffee you use. Do I really want to buy a bunch of different instant coffees to see which is best? I'll think about it.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
commentBody: Most recipes call for equal parts sugar, coffee, and water. This is good, but as a normal black coffee drinker, it’s just not for me.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
commentBody: Have made this numerous times since it was published a few weeks ago in NYT. An immersion blender with the accompanying container works wonders. Now to get Illy instant coffee available in the US, it will take this into the stratosphere, maybe ionosphere.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
commentBody:
I followed the recipe to the letter using electric hand mixer.  Doubled the amounts to ensure I'd have enough for two "drinks."  Ended up with enough for about 8-10 drinks.  I found that the whipped coffee didn't mix easily with the milk.  All in all, meh.  

BTW, IMHO, your ratings should be revised to:
1 star - Not worth it
2 stars - OK but nothing special
3 stars - Good
4 stars - Very Good
5 stars - Delicious or excellent

Right now, you have 2 stars=fine but fine > good so it's nonsense

author: Vaughn Vreeland
commentBody: I made with Folgers. Terrible!

author: Vaughn Vreeland
commentBody: Me too - the only decaf I could find. It was pretty bad.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
commentBody: This is a really delicious treat! It’s frothy and tasty. It has a lot of coffee in it, so I’d say this makes two drinks. The instant we’re using is 2 tsp for 12 oz coffee, so this recipe is like having 3 cups of coffee. I usually keep it to 2 cups a day, so depending on what you’reto, you might want to divide the recipe.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
commentBody: Used minimum 2T each sugar, espresso and boiling water, the ratio I found online. It makes me 2 servings. I've made larger batches (4T each) and frozen the extra to save time the next day or two. Mixed into whatever milk, cow or nut, it's delicious however I make it.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
commentBody: Needs more than 1/2 tsp sugar

author: Vaughn Vreeland
commentBody: Amazing recipe for a hot summer day. I sometimes add whipped cream, a spoon of vanilla ice cream, and/or a dash of salted caramel liquor.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
commentBody:
Save the calories; I used granulated Stevia. 1 Tbsp granulated Stevia = 1 Tbsp of sugar.

It's potent and delicious.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
commentBody: I used Mount Hagen coffee as well. I also cheated and used a tiny handheld milk frother to mix it up. This was my go-to post-run/pre-teach-French-2-over-Zoom drink during the past year. Highly recommend!

author: Vaughn Vreeland
commentBody: I couldn't get the right consistency with the proportions as listed. I had better luck with equal parts sugar, hot water, and instant coffee. 1 tablespoon of each did the trick

author: Vaughn Vreeland
commentBody: I use the Starbuck's instant Christmas blend, which has this wonderful spiciness, along with Nutpods cookie butter flavor, and Truvia or stevia instead of sugar. The Vietnamese make this drink with sweetened condensed milk. I'd always thought that was the original version.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
commentBody: I learned how to make this when I spent 24 hours or so in jail. It was delicious!

author: Vaughn Vreeland
commentBody: OMG I made this so often during COVID!  One of the better memories, it was delish and hit the spot...

author: Vaughn Vreeland
commentBody: I used 1.5 tsp instant coffee and it was still good!

author: Vaughn Vreeland
commentBody: Sub oat or almond milk for water

author: Vaughn Vreeland
commentBody: This was really good and took my Nescafe to a whole new place! Adjust amounts to your liking.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
commentBody: Oh yum.  That was delicious and SO easy.  I added whipping cream instead of milk because I had some on hand.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
commentBody: 1 tablespoon was plenty of coffee for one beverage. Using a hand held mixer made it easy and quick to prepare. Mixed into warm milk it was wonderful.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
commentBody: I used decaf - my stomach hates caffeine - it was yum!

author: Vaughn Vreeland
commentBody: Just here to say this worked equally as well with instant espresso powder (not granules), but used 1 T powder with 1 T sugar and 2 T hot water to serve two.

author: Vaughn Vreeland
commentBody: I tried this with a decent brand of instant coffee, and it just was so strong and bitter- it looks pretty, but it’s insanely concentrated. Maybe a very mild brand of coffee? I don’t think I get the appeal of this drink at all. I love coffee, but on the milder sweeter end.
